drug_class: Recombinant hepatitis B surface antigen vaccine
therapeutic_use: Prevention of hepatitis B virus infection

dosing_bands.duration_note: Birth dose within 24 hours; 0-1-6 month schedule (NHM) or 0-1-2-12 month accelerated
dosing_bands.notes: IM in anterolateral thigh; monovalent birth dose; preterm <2 kg: give at birth but do not count — restart 3-dose series at 1 month

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mL IM at 6, 10, 14 weeks (as pentavalent) or monovalent 0-1-6 months; 3-dose series
dosing_bands.notes: Anterolateral thigh in infants; deltoid in older children; check anti-HBs titre 1-2 months after last dose in high-risk

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mL (20 mcg) IM at 0-1-6 months; adult dose for >=16 years
dosing_bands.notes: Deltoid muscle; 3-dose series at 0, 1, 6 months

interactions.effect: Reduced immune response; may need higher dose or additional booster

contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous dose or vaccine component (yeast)
food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable vaccine)

therapeutic_use: Post-exposure prophylaxis — neonates born to HBsAg-positive mothers
Prevention of hepatitis B recurrence post liver transplant

dosing_bands.indication: Perinatal prophylaxis — neonate born to HBsAg+ mother
dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose within 12 hours of birth; give simultaneously with HepB vaccine at different site
dosing_bands.notes: Administer IM in anterolateral thigh opposite to vaccine site; must be given within 12 hours of birth for maximum efficacy

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose IM as soon as possible after exposure; concurrent HepB vaccine at different site
dosing_bands.notes: 0.06 mL/kg IM; give within 24 hours of percutaneous exposure, within 14 days of sexual exposure

interactions.effect: HBIG may interfere with immune response to live vaccines; defer live vaccines by 3 months after HBIG

contraindications: Known anaphylaxis to human immunoglobulin products
IgA deficiency with anti-IgA antibodies (risk of anaphylaxis)
food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable)

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=6 years for oral; injection used off-label in younger children

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg PO TDS for children 6-12 years; use for short-term relief only
dosing_bands.notes: For children >=6 years; swallow whole; not to be chewed; short-term use only (few days)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10-20 mg PO TDS; max 60 mg/day for adolescents >=12 years
dosing_bands.notes: Short-term use; consider if spasm is an ongoing concern — evaluate for underlying cause

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.3-0.5 mg/kg IV/IM per dose TDS; for severe colic not responsive to oral
dosing_bands.notes: Slow IV over 1 minute; monitor for hypotension; off-label in children <6 years

interactions.effect: Additive anticholinergic effects — dry mouth, urinary retention, constipation
Antagonistic effects on GI motility

food_instructions: Take before meals for best effect

unlicensed_note: Combination suspension licensed for children; use of alternating monotherapy preferred by IAP over fixed-dose combination

formulations.indian_conc_note: Ibuprofen 100 mg + Paracetamol 162.5 mg per 5 mL
Ibuprofen 50 mg + Paracetamol 125 mg per mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: Ibuprofen 5-10 mg/kg TDS (paracetamol dose proportional); use only if >3 months and >5 kg
dosing_bands.notes: Based on ibuprofen component; IAP recommends monotherapy alternation over FDC when possible; avoid in dehydrated children

dosing_bands.duration_note: Ibuprofen 5-10 mg/kg/dose TDS; max ibuprofen 400 mg/dose
dosing_bands.notes: Give after food; ensure adequate hydration; avoid in dengue, varicella, GI bleeding

interactions.effect: Increased risk of GI bleeding and ulceration
Enhanced anticoagulant effect; increased bleeding risk
Reduced renal clearance of methotrexate; increased toxicity
Reduced antihypertensive effect; risk of renal impairment

contraindications: Active GI bleeding or peptic ulcer
black_box_warnings: NSAIDs increase risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events, myocardial infarction, and stroke
NSAIDs increase risk of serious gastrointestinal adverse events including bleeding, ulceration, and perforation
food_instructions: Take after food to reduce gastric irritation

therapeutic_use: Status epilepticus refractory to other agents

dosing_bands.duration_note: Induction: 1.5-3% in O2 or O2/N2O; MAC higher in infants (1.6-1.87%)
dosing_bands.notes: MAC varies by age: neonate 1.6%, infant 1.87%, child 1.6%, adolescent 1.28%; use calibrated vaporizer only

dosing_bands.duration_note: Maintenance: 0.5-2.5% in O2 or O2/N2O; titrate to surgical depth
dosing_bands.notes: Continuous inhalation via calibrated vaporizer; monitor ETCO2, SpO2, ECG, BP; risk of malignant hyperthermia

interactions.effect: Increased risk of malignant hyperthermia
Potentiates neuromuscular blockade; reduce relaxant dose by 30-50%
Additive myocardial depression and hypotension

contraindications: Known or suspected susceptibility to malignant hyperthermia
History of confirmed hepatitis from halogenated anaesthetics
black_box_warnings: FDA warning: repeated or prolonged anaesthesia in children <3 years may affect brain development
food_instructions: NPO as per anaesthesia fasting guidelines (2h clear fluids, 4h breast milk, 6h solids)

formulations.indian_conc_note: Dextrose 5% + NaCl 0.22% + KCl 20 mEq/L; Na 25, K 20, Cl 22, Lactate 23 mEq/L

dosing_bands.duration_note: Holliday-Segar: 100 mL/kg/day for first 10 kg; infuse at 4 mL/kg/hr
dosing_bands.notes: CAUTION: Hypotonic fluids risk hyponatremia — monitor serum Na; WHO/IAP now recommends isotonic fluids (NS or Ringer's Lactate) for most sick children; Isolyte-P reserved for well children on maintenance

dosing_bands.duration_note: Holliday-Segar formula: first 10 kg = 100 mL/kg, next 10 kg = 50 mL/kg, thereafter 20 mL/kg; or 1500 mL/m2/day
dosing_bands.notes: Monitor electrolytes every 12-24 hours; switch to isotonic NS if hyponatremia develops; restrict to 2/3 maintenance in SIADH, meningitis, post-operative

interactions.effect: Risk of hyperkalemia due to K+ content (20 mEq/L)

contraindications: Conditions requiring isotonic fluid resuscitation (shock, DKA, burns)
black_box_warnings: Hypotonic IV fluids can cause fatal hyponatremia in children — monitor serum sodium closely
food_instructions: Not applicable (IV fluid)

drug_class: Inactivated Vero cell-derived Japanese encephalitis vaccine
therapeutic_use: NHM Universal Immunization Programme — endemic states
unlicensed_note: NHM recommends in endemic states; Haryana is NOT JE-endemic — not routinely given

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2 doses 28 days apart; first dose at 9-12 months with second dose at 16-24 months (NHM); or single dose live vaccine per campaign
dosing_bands.notes: Haryana is NOT JE-endemic — not routinely recommended; give only if travelling to endemic areas or on NHM campaign; SC or IM in anterolateral thigh/deltoid

interactions.effect: May reduce immune response to vaccine

contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous JE vaccine dose
food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable vaccine)

drug_class: Amino acid supplement / Nitric oxide precursor
unlicensed_note: Licensed for urea cycle disorders; GH stimulation test use is established but off-label

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading dose 200 mg/kg IV over 90 min, then continuous infusion 200-600 mg/kg/day; under metabolic specialist guidance
dosing_bands.notes: PICU setting only; monitor arterial pH, ammonia, arginine levels; contraindicated in arginase deficiency (argininemia)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 g/kg (max 30 g) IV over 30 min; blood sampling at 0, 30, 60, 90, 120 min for GH
dosing_bands.notes: Dilute in NS; infuse over 30 minutes; monitor blood glucose (risk of hypoglycemia); keep child fasting overnight

interactions.effect: Additive hypotensive effect
Arginine HCl is acidifying — may cause hyperkalemia in combination

black_box_warnings: Extravasation of IV arginine HCl causes severe tissue necrosis — ensure secure IV access
food_instructions: IV: NPO during infusion; Oral: take on empty stomach

unlicensed_note: Licensed for hypertension; pediatric use well-established but doses are off-label in many countries

dosing_bands.duration_note: Initial bolus 0.2-1 mg/kg IV over 2 min (max 40 mg); may repeat q10-15 min; or infusion 0.25-3 mg/kg/hr
dosing_bands.notes: PICU setting; continuous BP monitoring; reduce BP by no more than 25% in first 8 hours; supine during administration

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 1 mg/kg/dose BID; titrate up to 3 mg/kg/dose BID; max 10-12 mg/kg/day or 1200 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Monitor orthostatic BP; avoid abrupt withdrawal; may mask hypoglycemia symptoms

interactions.effect: Severe bradycardia, AV block, and heart failure risk
Masks hypoglycemia symptoms (tachycardia); prolonged hypoglycemia
Additive myocardial depression

contraindications: Second or third degree AV block
Pheochromocytoma without adequate alpha blockade (for oral use)
black_box_warnings: Severe hepatocellular injury reported — monitor LFTs; discontinue if liver injury suspected
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; food increases bioavailability

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1.25 mg (2.5 mL drops) once daily at bedtime; for ages 6 months to 2 years
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label below 2 years; use drops formulation; give at bedtime

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-5 years: 1.25 mg OD; 6-11 years: 2.5 mg OD; >=12 years: 5 mg OD
dosing_bands.notes: Give at bedtime; minimal sedation; reduce dose in renal impairment

interactions.effect: Additive sedation though levocetirizine is minimally sedating
Increased levocetirizine levels due to reduced renal clearance

food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; give at bedtime

drug_class: Selective beta-2 adrenergic agonist (R-isomer of salbutamol)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.31-0.63 mg via nebulization q6-8h; for infants <2 years
dosing_bands.notes: Half the dose of racemic salbutamol; less tachycardia; dilute to 3 mL with NS if needed

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.63-1.25 mg nebulization TDS-QDS; for children 2-12 years
dosing_bands.notes: Can be given q20 min × 3 doses in acute severe asthma; use 0.63 mg respule for mild-moderate, 1.25 mg for severe

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 puffs (50 mcg/puff) via spacer QDS PRN; equivalent to 100-200 mcg salbutamol MDI
dosing_bands.notes: Always use with spacer +/- facemask in children; shake well before use

interactions.effect: Antagonistic effect — beta-blockers reduce bronchodilatory action
Additive cardiovascular effects — tachycardia, hypertension
Additive hypokalemia risk

food_instructions: Not applicable (inhaled/nebulized); oral form may be taken with or without food

unlicensed_note: Widely used in Indian pediatric practice; not first-line — PEG/lactulose preferred per IAP

formulations.indian_conc_note: Liquid paraffin 3.75 mL + Milk of magnesia 11.25 mL per 15 mL
Sugar-free; with sodium picosulfate 3.33 mg/15 mL added

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5-10 mL at bedtime for children 3-12 years; not for prolonged use >2 weeks without review
dosing_bands.notes: Give at bedtime; not recommended <3 years (aspiration risk with liquid paraffin); PEG 3350 or lactulose preferred as first-line

dosing_bands.duration_note: 15-30 mL at bedtime; adjust dose to achieve soft stools
dosing_bands.notes: Short-term use preferred; chronic use may impair fat-soluble vitamin absorption; encourage dietary fiber and fluids

interactions.effect: Liquid paraffin impairs absorption of fat-soluble vitamins with chronic use
Give liquid paraffin at least 2 hours apart from other medications to avoid reduced absorption

food_instructions: Take at bedtime on empty stomach; do not give with meals (aspiration risk when recumbent)

therapeutic_use: Topical emollient for dry skin (external use)
unlicensed_note: Oral use not recommended in children <3 years due to aspiration risk; topical use safe at all ages

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-3 mL/kg/day in 1-2 divided doses; max 90 mL/day; for children >=3 years only
dosing_bands.notes: NOT for children <3 years (lipoid pneumonia risk); give between meals, not at bedtime in young children; PEG preferred as first-line

interactions.effect: Impairs absorption with chronic oral use
Do NOT co-administer — docusate increases absorption of liquid paraffin leading to granuloma formation

black_box_warnings: Aspiration of liquid paraffin causes severe lipoid pneumonia — do not give to children <3 years or those with swallowing difficulties
food_instructions: Give between meals; do not give at bedtime to young children due to aspiration risk in recumbent position

unlicensed_note: Licensed for use in children; preferred over prednisolone eye drops due to lower IOP elevation risk

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 drop in affected eye(s) QDS; 0.2% for allergic conjunctivitis, 0.5% for anterior uveitis; taper over 1-2 weeks
dosing_bands.notes: Shake well before use; preferred over dexamethasone/prednisolone in children (lower IOP risk); still monitor IOP in prolonged use >2 weeks

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5% suspension, 1-2 drops QDS; begin 24 hours post-surgery; taper gradually
dosing_bands.notes: Use 0.5% formulation for post-surgical inflammation; taper after 2 weeks to prevent rebound

interactions.effect: Wait at least 5 minutes between different eye drops
Remove contact lenses before instillation; wait 15 minutes before reinserting

contraindications: Viral eye infections (herpes simplex keratitis, vaccinia)
food_instructions: Not applicable (ophthalmic)

drug_class: Live attenuated measles virus vaccine (Edmonston-Zagreb strain)
unlicensed_note: Monovalent measles vaccine being replaced by MR/MMR in NHM; still used in outbreak response

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mL SC at 9 months (MCV1) and 16-24 months (MCV2) per NHM; now given as MR vaccine
dosing_bands.notes: SC in upper arm; use within 4 hours of reconstitution; protect from light; NHM has replaced with MR vaccine at 9 and 16-24 months

dosing_bands.duration_note: Can be given as early as 6 months in outbreak; does not count as MCV1 — still give at 9 months
dosing_bands.notes: Outbreak dose at 6 months does NOT count — regular MCV1 at 9 months still required; maternal antibodies may reduce seroconversion

interactions.effect: Defer measles vaccine 3-6 months after immunoglobulin administration (antibody interference)
Contraindicated in immunosuppressed — risk of vaccine-strain disease
Measles vaccine may suppress tuberculin reactivity for 4-6 weeks

contraindications: High-dose systemic corticosteroid therapy (>2 mg/kg/day for >14 days)
food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable vaccine)

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=6 months in India; widely used as antipyretic in Indian pediatric practice

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5-8 mg/kg/dose TDS; for infants >=6 months; max 3 days for fever
dosing_bands.notes: Give after food; avoid in dehydration, dengue; not first-line — use paracetamol/ibuprofen first

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5-8 mg/kg/dose TDS; max 500 mg/dose for older children
dosing_bands.notes: Give after food; limit to 7 days; diarrhea is common side effect; avoid in G6PD deficiency

interactions.effect: Increased bleeding risk; displaces warfarin from protein binding
Increased risk of GI bleeding; avoid concomitant use
Reduced lithium clearance; monitor levels
Reduced renal clearance of methotrexate

contraindications: Active GI bleeding or peptic ulcer
black_box_warnings: NSAIDs increase risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events
NSAIDs increase risk of serious gastrointestinal adverse events
Autoimmune hemolytic anemia reported with prolonged use (>7 days)
food_instructions: Take after food to reduce gastric irritation

formulations.indian_conc_note: Mefenamic acid 50 mg + Paracetamol 125 mg per 5 mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: Dose based on mefenamic acid component: 5-8 mg/kg TDS; max 3-5 days
dosing_bands.notes: Give after food; ensure total paracetamol from all sources <60 mg/kg/day; IAP recommends monotherapy first-line

interactions.effect: Risk of paracetamol overdose — hepatotoxicity; calculate total daily paracetamol
Increased bleeding risk from mefenamic acid component
Do not combine with other NSAIDs

black_box_warnings: Paracetamol overdose causes fatal hepatotoxicity — ensure total paracetamol from all sources <60 mg/kg/day
food_instructions: Take after food

therapeutic_use: Prevention of invasive meningococcal disease (serogroups A, C, W, Y)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-dose series 8-12 weeks apart starting at 9 months (Menactra); booster at 3 years if ongoing risk
dosing_bands.notes: Not in NHM routine schedule; indicated for high-risk: complement deficiency, asplenia, travel to endemic areas, outbreak

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose IM for age >=2 years; booster every 5 years if ongoing risk
dosing_bands.notes: Deltoid IM; IAP recommends for high-risk children; mandatory for Hajj/Umrah travel

interactions.effect: Reduced immune response; may need additional dose

contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous dose or vaccine component
Age <9 months (Menactra — risk of GBS with earlier dosing)
Current GBS history within 6 weeks of prior vaccination
food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable vaccine)

unlicensed_note: Used in Indian anaesthesia practice; limited pediatric data; phenylephrine/ephedrine preferred internationally

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1-0.4 mg/kg IV bolus; may repeat q10-15 min as needed; max cumulative 3 mg/kg
dosing_bands.notes: Slow IV push; monitor BP continuously; avoid in hypovolemic shock (volume resuscitation first); tachyphylaxis with repeated doses

interactions.effect: Severe hypertensive crisis — contraindicated
Increased risk of ventricular arrhythmias
Severe sustained hypertension when used together

food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable — emergency use)

unlicensed_note: Licensed for hypertension; pediatric SVT and heart failure use well-established but off-label

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 0.5-1 mg/kg/dose BID; titrate to effect; max 6 mg/kg/day or 200 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Start low and titrate slowly; monitor HR and BP; do not stop abruptly; may mask hypoglycemia

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg IV over 5 min; may repeat q5 min × 3; max total 5 mg
dosing_bands.notes: Slow IV push over 5 minutes with continuous ECG monitoring; have atropine and isoprenaline available

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mg/kg/dose TDS; for prevention of hypercyanotic spells until surgical correction
dosing_bands.notes: Crush and disperse in water for infants; critical bridge therapy until TOF repair; do not stop abruptly

interactions.effect: Severe bradycardia, AV block, heart failure — avoid IV combination
Rebound hypertension if clonidine withdrawn while on beta-blocker
Masks hypoglycemia symptoms; prolonged hypoglycemia
Additive bradycardia; monitor HR

contraindications: Second or third degree AV block
black_box_warnings: Do not abruptly discontinue — taper over 1-2 weeks to avoid rebound hypertension, angina, or arrhythmia
food_instructions: Take with or immediately after food; food enhances absorption

therapeutic_use: Ductus-dependent congenital heart disease — maintain PDA (off-label, resource-limited settings)
unlicensed_note: NOT licensed for pediatric use; oral misoprostol used off-label in resource-limited settings to maintain ductal patency when IV alprostadil unavailable

dosing_bands.indication: Maintain ductal patency — ductus-dependent CHD (when alprostadil unavailable)
dosing_bands.duration_note: 5-10 mcg/kg PO/SL/NG q3-6h; bridge therapy only — transfer to center with IV alprostadil
dosing_bands.notes: CRITICAL: Off-label use only when IV PGE1 unavailable; dissolve fragment of 200 mcg tablet in water; monitor SpO2, apnea, temperature; immediate transfer to cardiac center

dosing_bands.duration_note: 100-200 mcg QDS with food; for duration of NSAID therapy; rarely needed in children — PPI preferred
dosing_bands.notes: PPI (omeprazole) is preferred gastroprotective in children; misoprostol reserved for PPI-intolerant cases

interactions.effect: Additive uterotonic effect — not relevant in pediatrics but critical safety note
May worsen diarrhea — use aluminum-containing antacid instead

black_box_warnings: CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY — can cause abortion, premature birth, or birth defects
In neonatal ductal patency use: risk of apnea, hypotension — use only with monitoring and when IV PGE1 unavailable
food_instructions: Take with food (for gastroprotection indication); neonatal use: NG/sublingual

formulations.indian_conc_note: Montelukast 4 mg + Levocetirizine 2.5 mg per 5 mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-5 years: montelukast 4 mg + levocetirizine 2.5 mg OD at bedtime; 6-14 years: montelukast 5 mg + levocetirizine 2.5 mg OD
dosing_bands.notes: Give at bedtime; montelukast component: 4 mg for 2-5 yr, 5 mg for 6-14 yr; FDA black box on montelukast — discuss neuropsychiatric risk with parents

dosing_bands.duration_note: >=15 years: montelukast 10 mg + levocetirizine 5 mg OD at bedtime
dosing_bands.notes: Give at bedtime; counsel about neuropsychiatric risk (montelukast FDA black box); review need every 3 months

interactions.effect: Reduced montelukast levels — may decrease efficacy
Additive sedation from levocetirizine component

black_box_warnings: FDA black box: Montelukast — serious neuropsychiatric events including agitation, depression, sleep disturbances, suicidal thinking and behavior; evaluate risk vs benefit; counsel caregivers
food_instructions: Take at bedtime; may be taken with or without food

drug_class: Nutritional supplement — multivitamin, mineral, and amino acid complex

formulations.indian_conc_note: Multivitamin + zinc + lysine per 15 mL dose

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mL drops OD; for infants 6-12 months during/after illness
dosing_bands.notes: Drops formulation preferred for infants; give with meals; not a substitute for balanced nutrition

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5 mL (2-5 yr) to 10 mL (6-12 yr) syrup OD; for 1-3 months during recovery
dosing_bands.notes: Encourage dietary diversity; supplement is adjuvant only; check specific deficiencies (iron, vitamin D, zinc) and treat individually

interactions.effect: Some formulations already contain iron — check to avoid double supplementation
Mineral content (calcium, iron, zinc) may chelate antibiotics — separate by 2 hours

food_instructions: Take with or after meals for best absorption

drug_class: Opioid agonist-antagonist analgesic (kappa agonist, mu partial antagonist)
therapeutic_use: Reversal of opioid-induced pruritus (with retained analgesia)
unlicensed_note: Licensed for pediatric use; ceiling effect on respiratory depression makes it relatively safer than full agonists

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg IV/IM/SC q4-6h PRN; onset 2-3 min IV, 15 min IM
dosing_bands.notes: Ceiling effect on respiratory depression at ~30 mg; does not require controlled substance registration in India (Schedule H); may reverse mu-agonist analgesia if given with morphine/fentanyl

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg IV slow push q4-6h; monitor respiratory rate and SpO2
dosing_bands.notes: Neonatal use: start at lower dose; monitor closely for respiratory depression; NICU setting preferred

interactions.effect: Nalbuphine may partially reverse analgesia of full mu-agonists; may precipitate withdrawal in opioid-dependent patients
Additive respiratory depression and sedation
Unpredictable potentiation of opioid effects

contraindications: Head injury with raised ICP (may obscure neurological signs)
food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable)

unlicensed_note: Combination not licensed for pediatric use; individual components may be used separately in children; rarely needed in pediatric practice

dosing_bands.duration_note: Based on naproxen component: 5-7.5 mg/kg/dose BID; for adolescents >=12 years; max naproxen 500 mg/dose
dosing_bands.notes: Reserve for migraine with significant nausea; use individual components in younger children; domperidone max 10 mg TDS and max 7 days per EMA recommendation

interactions.effect: Domperidone prolongs QTc — additive risk of torsades de pointes
Increased domperidone levels — QT prolongation risk; contraindicated
Naproxen increases bleeding risk with anticoagulants
Naproxen reduces lithium clearance

contraindications: Known cardiac conduction abnormalities or QT prolongation
black_box_warnings: Domperidone: risk of serious cardiac arrhythmias (QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, sudden cardiac death); use lowest effective dose for shortest duration
NSAIDs increase risk of serious cardiovascular and gastrointestinal events
food_instructions: Take before meals; naproxen absorption is better with food but domperidone works best 15-30 min before food
